what_it_does: Defines PlatformSidebar, the left navigation rail for the platform UI kit. It renders the ConceptV wordmark, a 'Menu' header, a primary nav list of six items (NAV_ITEMS), and three collapsible Sections — Tools (four items plus a favourites slot), Integrations (collapsed, with View All and activation slots), and Support (collapsed, FAQs + Tutorials). Items call an onSelect(id) callback and highlight via an `active` prop. A local Section component provides the open/close collapse behaviour. The component is attached to window.
observations: NAV_ITEMS and TOOL_ITEMS are arrays of {id, glyph, label}; glyphs are emoji
Section is a small reusable collapsible with useState(open) and a chevron toggle (⌄ / ›)
Integrations and Support default to closed; Tools defaults open
Several slots show placeholder copy ('Favourited options appear here', 'Activated options appear here')
Integrations/Support buttons have no onClick wiring (inert), unlike NAV_ITEMS/TOOL_ITEMS
Uses inline style objects for layout (background:none, gap:4, marginTop:8, etc.) alongside cv- CSS classes
standouts: Two-tier nav model: a flat primary nav plus collapsible secondary Sections, with empty slots reserved for future favourited/activated items
conventions: Uses cv- prefixed class names and var(--bg-canvas) token reference, consistent with the design-system token discipline
Attaches component to window for global-script consumption
Mixes inline styles with token-driven classes — inline literals (gap:4, padding '0 14px') are off-token hardcodes
concerns: Inline style literals (gap, padding, marginTop) bypass the token system the project mandates (no raw px in consumers)
Integrations 'View All', FAQs, and Tutorials buttons have no onClick, so they are non-functional placeholders
Logo src is a relative file path that depends on mount location
notes: The platform left-rail: data-driven primary nav + collapsible Tools/Integrations/Support sections with reserved empty slots, calls onSelect to drive screen routing.
questions: Should the inline px/gap literals be tokens per the project's no-raw-px rule, or are these intentional prototype shortcuts?
Are the Integrations/Support buttons meant to be wired to onSelect like the others?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — comment says 'left nav rail' and it is the navigation rail driving screen selection.
apparent_themes: Data-driven navigation (config arrays render the items)
Reserved slots for future personalization (favourites, activated integrations)
intention_signals: Aims to present a full product navigation rail mirroring the screen catalogue, with collapsible grouping and placeholders for personalization features not yet built.
novelty: The reserved-slot placeholders ('Favourited options appear here') that pre-shape where future user-customized nav entries will land.
contribution: Contributes a two-tier navigation taxonomy (primary nav + Tools/Integrations/Support groups) and the collapsible Section primitive, plus the glyph+label nav item config.
summary_for_embedding: React PlatformSidebar nav rail rendering a six-item primary nav plus collapsible Tools/Integrations/Support sections, driving screen selection via onSelect.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the platform's left navigation rail mirroring the product screen catalogue, with collapsible groups and reserved slots for future personalization.
